variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_ct/ct.py — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case is two consecutive lines (a and b — strings, may be empty). For each case, output 'true' if a == b else 'false', on its own line. Implement a constant-time comparison: loop over all bytes (length-padded, length mismatch sets a 'differ' flag) and OR a XOR-accumulator. Do NOT short-circuit on first difference. Do NOT call any stdlib constant-time / timing-safe equality helper. Pure Python; no numpy.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_ct; ! grep -qE "hmac\\.compare_digest|secrets\\.compare_digest" ct.py || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; python3 ct.py < input.txt > out.txt; diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_ct/ct.zig — Reads from stdin: line 1 is T. Each case is two consecutive lines (a and b — strings, may be empty). For each case, output 'true' if a == b else 'false', on its own line. Implement a constant-time comparison: loop over all bytes (length-padded, length mismatch sets a 'differ' flag) and OR a XOR-accumulator. Do NOT short-circuit on first difference. Do NOT call any stdlib constant-time / timing-safe equality helper. Use Zig 0.16+ idioms. Entrypoint: `pub fn main(init: std.process.Init) !void`. For stdio, the rich API (`takeDelimiter`, `appendRemainingUnlimited`, `print`, `writeAll`, `flush`) lives on the `std.Io.Reader`/`Writer` *interface*, NOT on the concrete `std.Io.File.Reader`/`Writer`. Reach the interface via `&fr.interface`. Canonical setup: `var in_buf: [4096]u8 = undefined; var fr = std.Io.File.stdin().reader(init.io, &in_buf); const r = &fr.interface;` and the symmetric writer. Then `try r.takeDelimiter('\n')` (returns `?[]u8`, null at EOF) for line-by-line, or `try r.appendRemainingUnlimited(init.arena.allocator(), &list)` to slurp. Always `try w.flush()` before return — output is buffered. Strict-default footguns (errors, not warnings, in 0.16): rename unused params to `_`, discard unused locals with `_ = x;`, prefer `const` over `var` when not mutated. Use `std.Io` (capital I), not `std.io`. Compile with `zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast ct.zig`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_ct; ! grep -qE "std\\.crypto\\.timing_safe|std\\.crypto\\.utils\\.timingSafeEql" ct.zig || { echo 'forbidden api'; exit 1; }; zig build-exe -O ReleaseFast ct.zig && ./ct < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
